sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.83 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: We say that an infinite simple group$G$is a monster of the third kind\/ if for every non-trivial elements$a,b$, of which at least one is not an involution, there are infinitely many elements$g G$such that$< a,b^g > =G$. (Compare with V.\,P.\,Shunkov's definitions in Archive,~6.63, 6.64.) Is it true that every simple quasi-Chernikov group is a monster of the third kind? This is true for quasi-finit...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
